macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Stocks fall as oil rises and bond yields stay in focus
items.summary: U.S. equities weakened while crude prices climbed, with investors watching Treasury yields and inflation risks tied to higher energy costs. Market commentary highlighted concern that a renewed oil shock could keep rates elevated and pressure stocks.

items.title: Global economy seen as more fragile amid geopolitical and inflation risks
items.summary: A macro outlook discussion warned that conflict-related disruption to oil shipping could lift inflation worldwide and force interest rates higher. The analysis argued that financial markets remain vulnerable to shocks from energy, tariffs, and geopolitics.

items.title: University of Michigan says AI boom is supporting the U.S. economy
items.summary: The University of Michigan’s May 2026 outlook said the U.S. economy continued expanding in the first quarter, with AI-related investment helping bolster growth. The forecast also reflects ongoing attention to inflation, rates, and the durability of the expansion.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin drops below $77,000 as $657M–$661M in crypto positions are liquidated
items.summary: Bitcoin fell to around $76,700-$76,900, its weakest level in more than two weeks, amid a broad risk-off move in crypto markets. Across the network, roughly $657 million to $661 million in liquidations were reported in 24 hours, with long positions taking the biggest hit.

items.title: Crypto market selloff intensifies as Ethereum slips near $2,100
items.summary: Ethereum and major altcoins including Solana and XRP also weakened in the latest crypto drawdown, with broad selling pressure hitting the sector. Market commentary cited higher Treasury yields and fading expectations for near-term Fed rate cuts as key macro headwinds.

items.title: CLARITY Act momentum seen as priced in after Senate Banking Committee approval
items.summary: Crypto market coverage noted that the CLARITY Act passed the Senate Banking Committee by a 15-9 vote, a major step toward a comprehensive U.S. digital asset regulatory framework. Traders, however, appeared to treat the development as largely priced in rather than an immediate bullish catalyst.

items.title: Crypto NewsNow roundup highlights ETH weakness, bridge exploit, and corporate treasury buying
items.summary: A live crypto news feed tracked several notable developments, including Ethereum weakness, an $11.58 million cross-chain exploit involving the Verus-Ethereum bridge, and continued corporate accumulation of ETH. The roundup also flagged ongoing activity in crypto treasury strategies and regulated product launches.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Nintendo announces Pictonico, a mobile game that turns photos into mini-games
items.summary: Nintendo has revealed Pictonico, a new mobile title that uses users’ photos as the basis for mini-games. The app is scheduled to launch later this month.

items.title: Ghost of Yotei: Legends has received its last major update
items.summary: Sucker Punch has confirmed that Ghost of Yotei: Legends will not receive any more major content updates. The multiplayer mode will remain playable, but future support appears limited to maintenance.

items.title: Subnautica 2 launches strong in early access with massive player counts
items.summary: Subnautica 2 has gotten off to a very strong start, earning solid early reviews and drawing huge concurrent player numbers. Developers have also shared an early access roadmap outlining substantial upcoming additions.

items.title: Amazon reportedly cancels its Lord of the Rings MMO
items.summary: Reports say Amazon has canceled its Lord of the Rings MMO, a project that had already been affected by layoffs in Amazon’s games division. The move marks another setback for the company’s internal gaming ambitions.
items.source: YouTube / gaming news recap citing reported coverage

items.title: Two unannounced Xbox controllers reportedly surface
items.summary: Microsoft appears to be preparing new Xbox hardware accessories, including a possible Elite Series 3 controller and a cloud-focused pad. The devices have surfaced in recent reporting, but Microsoft has not officially announced them.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 continues to dominate discussion after early access launch
items.summary: Forza Horizon 6 is drawing major attention as one of 2026’s biggest releases, with reports of strong early access interest and high player counts. Reviews and previews highlight its expanded world, multiplayer systems, and seasonal live events.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Google’s latest non-AI app icon redesign is rolling out
items.summary: Google is rolling out updated app icons with a more modern look and clearer visual distinction between products. The change appears to be part of a broader refresh across Google’s consumer apps.

items.title: Sony’s luxury 10th-anniversary ColleXion headphones leak
items.summary: A leak shows Sony’s upcoming anniversary headphones with extra padding, polished metal accents, and studio-oriented sound branding. The premium design suggests Sony is targeting the high-end audio market with a special-edition product.

items.title: Sony raises PlayStation Plus prices for one- and three-month plans
items.summary: Sony is increasing PS Plus pricing for shorter subscription tiers, citing ongoing market conditions. The move affects players who prefer monthly or quarterly access over annual plans.

items.title: Microsoft is removing Teams’ “Together mode”
items.summary: Microsoft is discontinuing Together mode in Teams, saying it adds too much implementation complexity. The company will instead steer users toward the standard gallery view for meetings.

items.title: New crash data raises questions about Tesla robotaxi safety
items.summary: Fresh data indicates two crashes since July 2025 involving Tesla robotaxis while remote operators were controlling the vehicles. The reports add to scrutiny over the safety and oversight of autonomous ride services.

items.title: Google I/O 2026 kicks off with major product announcements expected
items.summary: Google’s keynote is set to highlight a slate of new product news, with Android XR updates also possible. The event is likely to be one of the week’s biggest technology announcements.
